text: Saguaro National Park
zh: 萨瓜罗国家公园

text: A monarch.
zh: 帝王之姿。

text: The saguaro.
zh: 萨瓜罗巨人柱。

text: King of all cactuses.
zh: 仙人掌之王。

text: And the royal icon of the American Southwest.
zh: 美国西南部的皇家象征。

text: Here in the Sonoran Desert is the only place on Earth where the saguaro grows.
zh: 在索诺兰沙漠，这里是地球上唯一生长萨瓜罗巨人柱的地方。

text: The home of Saguaro National Park, near the Arizona -Mexican border, there are 91 ,000 protected acres bisected by the city of Tucson.
zh: 萨瓜罗国家公园地处亚利桑那州与墨西哥边境附近，占地9.1万英亩，图森市从中穿过。

text: sun.
zh: 阳光。

text: In this park, the saguaro is a thirsty monarch, sucking up as much as 200 gallons of water during a rare but torrential desert rainstorm.
zh: 在这座公园里，萨瓜罗巨人柱是个口渴的君王，在难得一见却倾盆而下的沙漠暴雨中，能吸收多达200加仑的水。

text: These royal giants grow slowly, but can reach 50 feet in height and reign through a lifespan span of a hundred and fifty years.
zh: 这些巨大的植物生长缓慢，却能长到50英尺高，寿命长达150年。

text: Solid as they appear to be, it is water that makes up most of their weight.
zh: 它们看起来坚实无比，但体重的大部分其实都是水分。

text: They are the most recognized cactus in the world.
zh: 它们是世界上最容易辨认的仙人掌。

text: The saguaro has become a symbol of the American West.
zh: 萨瓜罗巨人柱已成为美国西部的象征。

text: Park visitors head out on the Cactus Forest Trail, a desert thoroughfare much used by hikers and bicycles.
zh: 游客们沿着仙人掌森林步道前行，这是一条深受徒步者和骑行者喜爱的沙漠通道。

text: Hikers must avoid brushing against the spines of the low -growing, shovel -like cholla cactus.
zh: 徒步者必须小心，避免碰到低矮如铲子般的多刺仙人掌。

text: They bristle with barbed spines, which are painfully difficult to remove once embedded in your flesh.
zh: 它们布满倒钩状的尖刺，一旦扎入皮肉，拔除起来痛苦不堪。

text: Many desert plants bristle.
zh: 许多沙漠植物都长满尖刺。

text: The agave.
zh: 龙舌兰。

text: Water is so scarce that cactuses protect the water they have stored with sharp spines.
zh: 水源极其稀缺，仙人掌只能靠尖刺来保护体内储存的水分。

text: The prickly pear.
zh: 仙人梨。

text: Without these spines, insects, birds, and animals would eat them.
zh: 没有这些尖刺，昆虫、鸟类和动物早就把它们吃光了。

text: At first glance, this expansive desert seems lifeless.
zh: 乍一看，这片辽阔的沙漠似乎毫无生机。

text: Too much sun.
zh: 阳光太烈。

text: Too little rain.
zh: 雨水太少。

text: But a longer look reveals a profusion of wildlife.
zh: 但仔细观察，就会发现这里其实生机勃勃。

text: the constant scarcity of water,
zh: 面对持续的水资源匮乏，

text: each creature struggles endlessly to stay alive.
zh: 每一种生物都在不断挣扎求生。

text: Water is life.
zh: 水就是生命。

text: The wriggle saguaro provides sweet fruit for hungry creatures and homes to birds,
zh: 扭曲的萨瓜罗巨人柱为饥饿的动物提供甘甜的果实，也为鸟类提供栖身之所，

text: such as the Harris Hawk and the elf owl, the smallest owl in existence.
zh: 比如哈里斯鹰和地球上最小的猫头鹰——精灵鸮。

text: Like all deserts, Saguaro has extreme fluctuations in daily temperatures.
zh: 和所有沙漠一样，萨瓜罗国家公园的昼夜温差极大。

text: Cloudless skies and unshaded ground make a blast furnace of the daytime.
zh: 晴空万里、地面毫无遮蔽，白天就像一座熔炉。

text: While at night, without cloud cover or moisture to hold the heat, bitter cold takes over.
zh: 而到了夜晚，没有云层或水汽保温，刺骨的寒冷便会袭来。

text: Some creatures burrow underground or crawl into rock dens to escape the killer sun of summer and the cold nights of winter.
zh: 一些动物会钻入地下或岩穴，以躲避夏日的酷热骄阳和冬夜的严寒。

text: When summer temperatures soar to over 100 degrees Fahrenheit,
zh: 当夏季气温飙升至华氏100度以上时，

text: scorpions exude a coating of wax to seal in moisture, an effective barrier against dehydration.
zh: 蝎子会分泌一层蜡质来锁住体内水分，有效阻止脱水。

text: The only known year -round spring is found at King Canyon in the Tucson Mountains.
zh: 图森山脉的国王峡谷，是这里唯一已知的常年不断的泉水所在地。

text: This reliable source of water is often frequented by the collared peccary, a harried wild mammal.
zh: 这处可靠的水源常常吸引一种奔波劳碌的野生哺乳动物——领西貒前来。

text: They're also known by their Spanish name, javelina.
zh: 它们还有个西班牙语名字，叫javelina。

text: When not sleeping during the heat of day, these members of the New World pig family are found rooting among beds of prickly pear.
zh: 这些属于新大陆猪科的动物，白天酷热时多在休息，其余时间常在仙人梨丛中拱土觅食。

text: They're social animals, usually traveling in herds.
zh: 它们是群居动物，通常成群结队地活动。

text: The king of cactus and profuse wildlife aren't the park's only attractions.
zh: 仙人掌之王和丰富的野生动物并非公园唯一的看点。

text: Red Hills Visitor Center in the Tucson Mountain District offers an insight to the Native American take on the saguaro cactus.
zh: 图森山区的红崖游客中心，展现了美洲原住民对萨瓜罗仙人掌的独特理解。

text: Signal Hill is well worth the hike.
zh: 信号山这段徒步路程绝对值得一走。

text: Here, some thousand years ago, near what is today an inviting picnic area, Native Americans left hundreds of petroglyphs.
zh: 大约一千年前，就在如今这片宜人的野餐区附近，美洲原住民留下了数百幅岩画。

text: Though we have never deciphered the meaning of these pictorial records,
zh: 尽管我们始终未能破译这些图画记录的含义，

text: we feel certain that the prehistoric people who made them hiked in these forests of the Saguaro and groves of Mesquite, probing the secrets of the desert as we do today.
zh: 但可以肯定的是，创作它们的史前先民曾在这片萨瓜罗森林和牧豆树丛中穿行，探索沙漠的秘密，正如我们今天所做的一样。

text: Thus, this seemingly dead earth reveals itself to be a land of life.
zh: 于是，这片看似死寂的土地，展露出它作为生命之地的真容。
